item.title: backlog history row limit flag to cap output
item.intent: Users must be able to see just the most recent N sprint completions without piping through head — a limit flag makes history browsable at any archive size.
item.description: cmd_backlog_history() reads the full sprints.jsonl and prints all matching rows. For projects with many sprints, the output can be hundreds of rows. The command has no way to cap the output to N most recent entries. Adding a --limit N flag would print only the last N entries (after applying any --since or --failed filters). This is analogous to git log -n.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_history() and add --limit to the flag parsing loop; add local limit=0 before the loop
In the --limit case: validate_arg then set limit=$2; shift 2; validate that the value is a positive integer
After filtering and collecting results, if limit > 0, pipe through tail -n $limit to keep only the last N entries
Add --limit to the usage message in cmd_backlog_history and cmd_help
Verify: 'backlog history' with limit 5 prints at most 5 rows; without limit flag prints all rows
acceptanceCriteria: backlog history with a limit flag prints only the N most recent matching entries
backlog history with both --since and a limit flag applies date filter first then limits rows
backlog history with no limit flag still prints all entries unchanged
